ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: M le teeri nɔiyeŋka ale bo mi ale fi ale ŋanvuta meena tɔgatɔga la suŋsuŋ. Nyiam kan ŋman lik tɛŋka a ko ŋanvuta meena.
translated: I will tell you about how we used to celebrate Christmas in the past. People did not know about Christmas at that time.

ref: The rainbow will be the sign of that solemn promise. Noah and His Family
text: Dila Wen yaa weeni Nowa ayen, <<Jabuui ale a sak nɔiyeŋka ate mi nye mi ale fi ale ŋanvuta meena tɛŋka zuk la ale nna.>>
translated: The Lord said to Noah, "Go into the ark, you and all your household, for I have seen that you are righteous before me in this generation."

ref: All people on earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
text: Nowa nidoabilisa batawa jam le la. Bala abe le biak ate nuruma waaŋ tɛŋka meena.
translated: Now they have become very many. They are in all the countries of the world.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Keenan kowa Yɛm yaa nya wa kowa chaboalaŋa a yaa weeni wa suataŋa bayewa ale bo peelim la.
translated: Keenan and Yɛm went to see the children who had come to the party and they greeted them.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Alege Sɛm ale Jafet yaa pai garuk a vi ba ŋmankpala zuk a cheŋ tuntula a ga lik ba kowa chaboalaŋa. Ba jam tulim ba ninaŋa a basi ka nakpiak ayen ba kan nya ba kowa chaboalaŋa.
translated: The sons of Shem and Japheth took a garment and laid it across their shoulders and walked in backwards and covered their father's nakedness. Their faces were turned away so they did not see their father's nakedness.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Nowa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale nna. Nyiamu ale jam lik tɛŋka la ŋaaŋnyiŋ ba yaa jam chim ka tɛŋ kpieŋsa a waaŋ tɛŋka meena zuk.
translated: Now listen to this. Any animal that was living in the land heard that they were going to choose a land chief so all the land animals gathered.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Alege Naawen yaa basi ate nyiŋtui kpiema a yik Faaro ale wa yenni demma Abram powa Sarai nyiŋ.
translated: When Pharaoh's officials saw her beauty, they told Pharaoh and took Sarai to Pharaoh's house.

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: Dula mɛ ale wa liŋ a sɛ kuŋkuŋku la. Dula jigini Abram yaa wi Naawen yuenni.
translated: The man was very rich. The man's name was Abram and his wife's name was Naomi.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: M le basi ate fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa a piisi ase tintain busuŋ la, ate waai a dan baga a chiini tintain busuŋku, wa le baga a chiini fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa.
translated: I will go and tell your parents about your bad behavior, and if they don't correct your bad ways, they will go and tell your parents.

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Dila nyiŋ Abram yaa torisi wa viitaŋa a cheŋ moata Mamre ale bo Yebron la tiib kpieŋsaŋa jigini. Dula jigini, wa yaa sɛ kuŋkuŋ a te Naawen.
translated: One day Abram moved his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, and he built an altar there for the Lord.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: Dila po Sinar naawa Amrapel ale Ellasar naawa Ariok ale Iilam naawa Chedorlaomer ale Goyim naawa Tidal,
translated: In the days of Shinar's king Amraphel and Ellasar's king Ariok and Elam's king Chedorlaomer and Goiim's king Tidal,

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Bena pi ale ŋanaansiwa po, Chedorlaomer ale nalimaŋa yaa nye nɔiyeŋ a cheŋ ga nak dɛ Arefa demma ale bo Asterot Karnaim la ale Zuzi demma ale bo Yɛm la ale Emi demma ale bo Saveya Kiriataim la
translated: In the fourteenth year, Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him came and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth Karnaim, and the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh Kiriathaim

ref: At Siddim Valley, the armies of the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bela fought the armies of King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Babylonia, and King Arioch of Ellasar. The valley
text: Ayen ba siti Iilam naawa Chedorlaomer ale Goyim naawa Tidal ale Sinar naawa Amrapel ale Ellasar naawa Ariok. Nalima ŋanaansi ale ta chaab ale nalima ŋanu.
translated: In the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goiim. These four kings against five kings.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Siddim Viaka jam ta ka gola ate ŋa sueri ale kotaali. Sodom ale Gomora nalimaŋa ale jam a chali la, nuruma gela yaa lo ŋa po alege nuru mbala chali jueli guutaŋa zuima.
translated: The Valley of Siddim was full of tar pits. When the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some of their men fell into the pits, while the rest escaped to the hills.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Nalima ŋanaansiwa yaa vari Sodom ale Gomora kɔlimaŋa meena ale ba ŋandiintaŋa meena a ta cheŋ.
translated: The punishment that came upon Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighbors and their surroundings is still there.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Abram le wom ayen wa suoku nidoabinni ba yik ta cheŋ la, wa yaa wi nuruma kobsita ale pi ale naanika ate ba biak ba wa yenni po a sak ba la a nyini ta ba a ga paari Dan.
translated: Abram took his wife Sarai and his nephew Lot, and all the possessions they had accumulated and the people they had acquired in Haran, and they set out for the land of Canaan, and they arrived there.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Ate wa yaa niak Abram ayen, <<Wen Dii Ale Bo Wenŋmazuk La a niak Abram, wa ka waai ale nye wenzuk ale tɛŋ.
translated: When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to Abram, "The Lord God of Heaven said to Abram, you will give me your wife and the land."

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: Abe niauŋ a bo Wen Dii Ale Bo Wenŋmazuk La jigi, wa ka waai ale pai fi dachaasaŋa a nyo fi nisima po.>> Abram yaa pai wa ŋantaŋa piika po bunyi a te wa.
translated: But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land before you, those you allow to remain will become barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides.>> Abram believed in his word and it was counted to him as righteousness.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: M kan siak a tuesi jaab fi jigiya alege ka mi nuruma ale dɛ ŋan baai la ale nuru baai ale va mu cheŋ la charuku. Bala ale Aner ale Eskol ale Mamre. Bala tuesi ba charuku.>>
translated: I will not give the young man anything except what the young men have eaten and the share of the men who went with me to war. Aner, Eshcol and Mamre. They will take their share.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Fi ŋaaŋbiik sii ale a saari bunaansiwa la ale ŋman jam de la, diila Amori demma biemu an diem zuak ya.>>
translated: Your daughters you can give to their sons and their daughters you can take for your sons, so that the Amorite people will become one people with you.>>

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Dai dila danni Naawen yaa nye nɔiyeŋ ale Abram ayen, <<M le pai tɛŋka de a te fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa a zaani Ejipiti benni a ga paari beli kpienni Yufiretis.
translated: On that day the Lord made a covenant with Abram, saying, "To your descendants I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates."

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Wa yaa weeni wa chorɔwa Abram ayen, <<Naawen a kasi mu kama ale bisa biam. Be goa ale m dɔkliewa ate m nya dɔkdem a nyini wa jigi.>> Abram yaa siak wa powa Sarai ale weeni diila.
translated: When they came near to Egypt, Abram said to his wife, "I know that you are a beautiful woman. When they see you, they will kill me but let you live." So Abram told Sarai to say she was his sister.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: M le basi ate fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa a piisi ate nɔiyeŋ kaai ate mi ale fi nye la a chim wensie.>>
translated: I will come and tell you my story and the things that happened to me so that you will know that I am telling you the truth.

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Abram yaa koori a pai diri a kpi tɛŋ, ate Wen weeni wa ayen,
translated: Abram was ninety-nine years old when God spoke to him,

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Ba kan ŋmaa wi fu ayen Abram. Fi yuenni yaa le chim ka Abrayam. Diila m basi kama ate fi chim tɛŋsa yega ko.
translated: They could not give birth to a child for Abram. His wife's name was Sarai and Abraham. That is why I changed his name to mean father of nations.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: M le basi ate m nɔiyeŋka a chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim mi ale fi ale fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa suŋsuŋ la. Ka le kasim boro kama ale fi ŋaaŋ sii ale cheen la. M le chim fi Wen ale fi ŋaaŋsaŋa Wen.
translated: I will tell you about my work and the work we do together in our community. We help people who are sick and suffering. I work with God and serve God.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Keenan tɛŋka meena ate fi boro yɔgyɔgla de a chim nichaanɔ nna, m le basi ate ka chim fi ale fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa tɛŋ kasim a cheŋ. M le chim ba Wen.>>
translated: The land of Canaan which I am giving you as your permanent possession, and I will be their God. I will be your God.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Ni gebi ni yoaŋa ate ku a sak nɔiyeŋka ale bo mi ale fi suŋsuŋ la.
translated: If you do not want to be a slave to sin, then you should not do what it wants.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, I saw that, although his wife Sarah had given birth to a son for him named Isaac, I also knew that I would make him a great nation that would be countless and would bless all the families of the earth through him.

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Wen ale biisi ale Abrayam nueri la, wa yaa jueli wen ale ge wa.
translated: When those people saw Abraham's face, they were very happy to see him.

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: Saara ale poli ku wari la, wa yaa la wa poi po, ayen mi yaa le jiak dii ale ge ate m dɔk nidoawa mɛ a kpaŋ la, m le nya masimu de a dɛ?
translated: If Sarah is not at home, and she has gone to the farm, how can I go and meet her there to tell her the message that her husband sent, can I get a vehicle to go there?

ref: When the three men got ready to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked part of the way with them.
text: Nuruma ale yiti ayen ba basi dula la, ba yaa nya Sodom geŋsa. Abrayam yaa a cheŋ ale ba ayen wa basi ba suik.
translated: When the men got up to leave, they looked toward Sodom. Abraham was walking with them to see them off.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Nuru baai ale za tuoku nɔi la, bimbaan paaluk ale ŋankpagsa ba yaa basi ate ba yi a zɛ tuoku jigi ya.
translated: When Nuru and his friends were going to the farm, the children asked their grandfather to allow them to go to the farm too.

ref: The two angels said to Lot, “The Lord has heard many terrible things about the people of Sodom, and he has sent us here to destroy the city. Take your family and leave. Take every relative you have in the city, as well as the men your daughters are going to marry.”
text: dii nyiŋla ti a yaa ti kaasi dela jigini kama. Ba le kaari dii a te Naawen nuruma ale nye diila a zuak kama ate wa tom ti ayen ti kaasi ka.
translated: The person who goes to the market should not forget his home. They are saying that he is in God's hands and whatever happens to him, he should accept it as it is.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Ate Naawen a basi ate bolim kaala a nyini wenŋmazuk a siŋ Sodom ale Gomora zuk.
translated: But Lot's wife looked back and became a pillar of salt like Sodom and Gomorrah.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Lɔti liema meena yaa jam yik puusa ba le goa ale wa la.
translated: The child's mother went to the market to buy things for him/her.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Alege Wen yaa jam Abimelek jigi gɔdaasima po yok kunyi a yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Fi a nye ase ka kpio la, dii nyiŋla nipowa ate fi ta la ka nipok waai ale yali.>>
translated: When Abimelech's father's servants had dug a well and he went to see it, he said to him, "If you are a man and strong, let the people know that you dug this well so that people will always remember you."

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Abimelek yaa wi Abrayam ate wa jo a weeni wa ayen, ka boan jaab ate fi nye ti dila? Mi kaasi fu ka sɛ ate fi ta kaasuŋku de jam mi ale m tɛŋka zuwa? Fi nye mu ka wie ŋaai ale kan magsi fi nye.
translated: Abimelech said to Abraham, "What have you done to us? How have I wronged you that you have brought such great guilt upon me and my kingdom? You have done things to me that should not be done."

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Ate wa yaa ŋman gum du ayen, <<Ka wana daam ate ni wa weeni Abrayam ayen biik ale ŋoosi Saara? Alege mi a biak nidoabili m kpaŋsa po a te Abrayam.
translated: And when she saw that she had conceived, <<Did she not say to herself that she was like Abraham's wife Sarah? Therefore I have given birth to a son for my husband who is Abraham.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: M le basi ate dɔkliewa nidoabinni a chim tɛŋ mɛ dii nyiŋla wa ka fi buuri.>>
translated: I am happy that the doctors are now able to treat the disease in our country so that people don't die again.

ref: Then she sat down a long way off, because she could not bear to watch him die. And she cried bitterly.
text: Wa yaa cheŋ niŋ nna maga a ga kala. Wa yaa poli ayen, <<M kan baga a nya nidoabinni ate wa a kpi.>> Wa le kala a moata dula la wa yaa piilim kumsa.
translated: He went and took the thing and put it in his bag. He thought to himself, "I will give it to my wife when I arrive home." He put the bag on his head and started walking quickly.

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Wen yaa lak wa nina ate wa nya vili ale nyiam. Wa yaa cheŋ a ga sueri bunlɔkku ale nyiam a yaa te nidoabinni ate wa nyu.
translated: When a woman gives birth to a child, she becomes very happy. She will search for good food for the child so that it will grow well.

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
text: Abrayam yaa ta piisa ale niiga a jam te Abimelek ate ba nɔi bayewa a nye nɔiyeŋ.
translated: Abraham lived in the land of the Philistines and stayed with Abimelech and his people for a long time.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Ku yaa jam paari diipo ate Wen a nye Abrayam a nya. Wa weeni wa ayen, <<Abrayam.>> Abrayam tulisi ayen, <<Mi ale nna.>>
translated: It was not long after that God called Abraham. When He called him, He said, "Abraham." Abraham answered Him, "Here I am."

ref: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
text: Wen yaa weeni ayen, <<Pai fi nidoabinni, fi bi yeŋka, Aizik waai ate fi a yaali la, a ta cheŋ Moria tɛŋka a kaab wa dula ase juka kaabka la guutaŋa kunyi zuk ate m le weeni fu la.
translated: When he had said this, "Take your only son, whom you love, Isaac, and go to the land of Moriah and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains that I will show you.

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
text: Vari ale veenti la saluik yokyokwa, Abrayam yaa yiti a gomsi wa boniŋ. Wa yaa basi ate yɔmma baye ale wa nidoabinni Aizik a va wa. Wa le yaali daata a magsi juka kaabka la, wa yaa lo suik po a cheŋ juijui ate Wen a weeni wa la.
translated: When he was very old, Abraham had received all his blessings. He had lived to see his son Isaac whom he loved. He was now lying on his bed about to die, and he was very happy because God had blessed him.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Daa ŋatawa danni Abrayam yaa zak zuk a nya jigini nna niŋniŋŋa.
translated: And the servant gave Abraham all the good news.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Ba le paari juijui ate Wen a weeni la, Abrayam yaa sɛ kuŋkuŋ dula a dueni daataŋa ku zuk a magsi. Wa yaa bobi wa nidoabinni Aizik a dueni kuŋkuŋku zuk daataŋa zuk.
translated: When Abraham was very old and the Lord had blessed him, Abraham told his chief servant, the one in charge of all that he had, to put his hand under his thigh and swear to him.

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Wa nɔŋku waai yuei jam le Areuma la mɛ a biak ka nidoabilisa. Ba yue jam le Teba ale Gayam ale Tayas ale Maaka.
translated: His wife bore him four sons in Arumah and they were all warriors. The four sons were Teba and Gayam and Tayas and Maaka.

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: <<Wom ti le a weeni diila. Fi ka nabiik kaai ale zuak a bo le ti. Lueri tama vieŋa bunyi a gu fi powa. Ti waai a kan zɛri ayen fi kan gu fi kpiewa, wa vɔrup po.>>
translated: A person who is not your friend. You and your children should not go near them. The world is full of evil things that can harm you. We want you to know what you can do to protect yourself, be careful.

ref: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
text: ate wa pa Makpela vɔrumu ate wa soa la ate bu bo talimu kpeglimka jigini la a da te mu. Ni weeni wa ate wa pai da bu le magsi diila a te mu ate ku chim guka jigi ni tɛŋka po.>>
translated: in the cave of Machpelah which is in the field which he bought from the Hittites as a burial place. And he commanded them that when he died they should bury him in his father's grave in his own land.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Tomteerɔwa yaa puusi ayen, <<Naawen yaa, mi kpagni Abrayam Wenni, te nakdeka jinla, abe fi sak ŋmanteka ale m kpagni Abrayam.
translated: The rich man also called out, saying, "Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, for I am in anguish in this flame."

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Abrayam tomteerɔwa yaa kpabi a tu wa a weeni ayen, <<Te mu nyiam maga dega fi liikka po.>>
translated: Abraham's servant knelt down and said, "Thank you God for showing me the way."

ref: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
text: Wa weeni gum du ayen, <<Ti ta wuuk kama a zuak, a ta dɔk mɛ a magsi fi goom yokku de.
translated: If you see a snake, <<We don't kill it and throw it away, we kill it and roast it to eat with our food.

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: Wa yaa weeni ayen, <<Mi ka Abrayam tomteerɔ.
translated: Before Abraham was born, <<I am.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: ate mi yaa koori tɛŋ a puusi Naawen, mi pak Naawen, mi kpagni Abrayam Wenni, waai ale basi ate mi nya suik maŋ a ga yaali mi kpagni suoku ŋaaŋ a te wa nidoabinni la.
translated: and I went to the land of Canaan, I left Canaan, I met Abraham's God, who said that I should come and he would give me my father's wealth that is in their hands.

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Tomteerɔwa yaa yeeri ligmɔniŋ ale kpana ŋanta ale gatta a te Aribeka. Wa pai ŋan baai diak ale pagra la tiirita a te wa toawa ale wa mawa.
translated: The servant told Isaac everything he had done, and Isaac took Rebekah and she became his wife and he loved her, and Isaac was comforted after his mother's death.

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ba yaa basi Aribeka suik ale wa dɔkliewa ale Abrayam tomteerɔwa ale wa nuruma.
translated: They took Rebekah and her maidservants and Abraham's servant and his men.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Ba yaa niak Aribeka ale weeni wa ayen, <<Ti toa, be nya niaka a paari tusa abe fi ŋaaŋsaŋa a tuesi ba dachaasaŋa nansuiŋtaŋa a tara.>>
translated: They said to Rebekah and her mother, "Let the girl stay with us a few more days, at least ten; after that she may go."

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: a bek tomteerɔwa ayen, <<Ka nuru boa le la a nyini peelimmu po a cheena ayen wa tu ti?>> Tomteerɔwa yaa tulisi ayen wa ka m kpagni. Wa yaa pai wa garukku a lik wadek.
translated: and asked the teacher, "Why do you put new wine in old wineskins?" The teacher answered and said to him. He took his bag and went home.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: Alege wa le jam diem vua la wa yaa te wa pooma nidoabilisaŋa tiirita a yaa basi ate ba va kori geŋka alege Aizik.

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”

translated: His parents thought that he was among his companions and they traveled for one day, then they searched for him. They did not find him, so they said, "Father, we have done something that we will regret for the rest of our lives." So they returned to go look for their child.
